records.title: Acme documentation
records.assistant_excerpt: The Acme documentation site is a pipeline-generated showcase that provides interactive references for five API protocols, automated quality evidence, and a KPI dashboard updated on every pipeline run.

records.title: REST API reference
records.assistant_excerpt: The Acme REST API provides 14 CRUD endpoints across five resources (projects, tasks, users, tags, and comments) over HTTP/1.1 with JSON payloads. This reference documents every endpoint, authentication flow, and error code.

records.title: GraphQL playground
records.assistant_excerpt: The Acme GraphQL API provides a single endpoint for flexible queries across projects, tasks, and users. This page documents the full schema, provides a live query editor, and covers authentication, error handling, and performance limits.

records.title: gRPC gateway invoke
records.assistant_excerpt: The Acme gRPC API provides high-performance Remote Procedure Call (RPC) access to project services over HTTP/2 with Protocol Buffers serialization. The HTTP gateway adapter allows you to invoke gRPC methods from any HTTP client without a gRPC library.

records.title: AsyncAPI event docs
records.assistant_excerpt: The Acme event system provides asynchronous, event-driven communication for project lifecycle changes. AsyncAPI 2.6.0 contracts define channel schemas, delivery guarantees, and payload formats for three event channels.

records.title: WebSocket event playground
records.assistant_excerpt: The Acme WebSocket API provides real-time, bidirectional communication for project updates and task notifications over a persistent connection. This playground allows you to connect, subscribe to channels, send messages, and observe server-pushed events.

records.title: Tutorial: launch your first Acme API integration
records.assistant_excerpt: This tutorial walks you through creating your first Acme API project, sending authenticated requests across three protocols (REST, GraphQL, and WebSocket), and verifying the integration works. You complete all five steps in under 15 minutes.

records.title: How-to: keep docs aligned with every release
records.assistant_excerpt: This guide shows you how to run the unified autopipeline on release day to ensure API documentation matches the latest contracts, passes quality gates, and generates a review manifest for operator approval. You complete the full flow in approximately 20 minutes.

records.title: Concept: pipeline-first documentation lifecycle
records.assistant_excerpt: Pipeline-first documentation is a methodology where automated systems generate, validate, and publish API documentation from source contracts. Humans review and approve outputs instead of writing from scratch, reducing review cycles from 5+ rounds to 1-2 rounds.

records.title: Troubleshooting: common Acme pipeline issues
records.assistant_excerpt: Your Acme documentation pipeline is failing with contract validation errors, quality gate warnings, or build failures. This guide fixes 95% of pipeline issues in under 5 minutes.

records.title: Quality evidence and gate results
records.assistant_excerpt: This page provides the complete quality evidence generated by the VeriDoc pipeline for the Acme documentation site. It covers KPI metrics, protocol gate results, 24 automated quality checks, RAG retrieval readiness, and all pipeline artifacts.
